content.text: Haystack is an open-source NLP framework from deepset built around a
abstraction. A pipeline is a directed acyclic graph (DAG) where each node is a
— a composable unit with typed input and output sockets. This explicitness makes large search and retrieval applications predictable and easy to validate at build time.

Haystack 2.x is a significant redesign from 1.x. It replaced monolithic pipeline classes with a generic graph that connects any components, enabling retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), question answering, and agentic flows in the same codebase.

pip install haystack-ai
pip install 'haystack-ai[cohere]'
pip install qdrant-haystack
pip install weaviate-haystack
A Python class with typed input/output sockets, decorated with @component
A DAG that connects components; validates at build time, executes at run time
The primary data unit: text, metadata, embedding, and optional score
Backend for storing and retrieving Documents (InMemory, Qdrant, Weaviate, ...)
Unlike LangChain or LlamaIndex, Haystack pipelines are validated before execution. If you connect an output socket with the wrong type to an input socket, Haystack raises a
at build time — not at runtime.
The following example builds a complete RAG pipeline: query embedding, vector retrieval, prompt building, and generation.

from haystack import Pipeline, Document
from haystack.document_stores.in_memory import InMemoryDocumentStore
from haystack.components.embedders import (
    SentenceTransformersTextEmbedder,
    SentenceTransformersDocumentEmbedder,
)
from haystack.components.retrievers.in_memory import InMemoryEmbeddingRetriever
from haystack.components.builders import PromptBuilder
from haystack.components.generators import OpenAIGenerator

store = InMemoryDocumentStore()
doc_embedder = SentenceTransformersDocumentEmbedder(model="BAAI/bge-small-en-v1.5")
doc_embedder.warm_up()

docs = [
    Document(content="The Eiffel Tower is located in Paris, France."),
    Document(content="The Colosseum is an ancient amphitheatre in Rome, Italy."),
    Document(content="The Great Wall stretches across northern China."),
]
docs_with_embeddings = doc_embedder.run(documents=docs)["documents"]
store.write_documents(docs_with_embeddings)

prompt_template = """
Answer the question using the context below.
Context:
{% for doc in documents %}{{ doc.content }}{% endfor %}
Question: {{ question }}
"""

pipeline = Pipeline()
pipeline.add_component("embedder", SentenceTransformersTextEmbedder(model="BAAI/bge-small-en-v1.5"))
pipeline.add_component("retriever", InMemoryEmbeddingRetriever(document_store=store, top_k=3))
pipeline.add_component("prompt_builder", PromptBuilder(template=prompt_template))
pipeline.add_component("llm", OpenAIGenerator(model="gpt-4o-mini"))

pipeline.connect("embedder.embedding", "retriever.query_embedding")
pipeline.connect("retriever.documents", "prompt_builder.documents")
pipeline.connect("prompt_builder.prompt", "llm.prompt")

result = pipeline.run({
    "embedder": {"text": "Where is the Eiffel Tower?"},
    "prompt_builder": {"question": "Where is the Eiffel Tower?"},
})
print(result["llm"]["replies"][0])
call links a named output socket on one component to a named input socket on another. The pipeline validates that socket types are compatible before any data flows.
becomes a Haystack component. Declare inputs via type annotations on
decorator declares what the component returns. Haystack uses this at build time to validate connections downstream.
Haystack supports multiple storage backends. The same pipeline code works with any store that implements the

from qdrant_haystack import QdrantDocumentStore

store = QdrantDocumentStore(
    url="http://localhost:6333",
    index="haystack_docs",
    embedding_dim=384,
    recreate_index=False,
)
In production, separate the indexing pipeline (runs once when documents change) from the query pipeline (runs on every user request).

from haystack import Pipeline, Document
from haystack.components.preprocessors import DocumentSplitter, DocumentCleaner
from haystack.components.embedders import SentenceTransformersDocumentEmbedder
from haystack.components.writers import DocumentWriter

indexing_pipeline = Pipeline()
indexing_pipeline.add_component("cleaner", DocumentCleaner())
indexing_pipeline.add_component(
    "splitter",
    DocumentSplitter(split_by="word", split_length=200, split_overlap=20),
)
indexing_pipeline.add_component(
    "embedder",
    SentenceTransformersDocumentEmbedder(model="BAAI/bge-small-en-v1.5"),
)
indexing_pipeline.add_component("writer", DocumentWriter(document_store=store))

indexing_pipeline.connect("cleaner.documents", "splitter.documents")
indexing_pipeline.connect("splitter.documents", "embedder.documents")
indexing_pipeline.connect("embedder.documents", "writer.documents")

raw_docs = [Document(content="...document text...")]
indexing_pipeline.run({"cleaner": {"documents": raw_docs}})
component that integrates with LLMs that support function-calling. Add tools as other components wrapped in

from haystack.components.agents import Agent
from haystack.components.tools import ComponentTool
from haystack.components.websearch import SerperDevWebSearch
from haystack.components.generators.chat import OpenAIChatGenerator

search_tool = ComponentTool(
    component=SerperDevWebSearch(api_key="..."),
    name="web_search",
    description="Search the web for current information.",
    input_mapping={"query": "query"},
    output_mapping={"documents": "results"},
)

agent = Agent(
    chat_generator=OpenAIChatGenerator(model="gpt-4o"),
    tools=[search_tool],
    system_prompt="You are a research assistant. Use web_search for recent events.",
    exit_conditions=["text"],
    max_agent_steps=10,
)

pipeline = Pipeline()
pipeline.add_component("agent", agent)

result = pipeline.run({
    "agent": {"messages": [{"role": "user", "content": "What happened in AI this week?"}]}
})
print(result["agent"]["messages"][-1]["content"])
Pipelines serialise to YAML for versioning, sharing, and reloading without recreating in code.

import yaml

# Save pipeline to YAML
with open("rag_pipeline.yaml", "w") as f:
    yaml.dump(pipeline.to_dict(), f)

# Reload
with open("rag_pipeline.yaml") as f:
    config = yaml.safe_load(f)

from haystack import Pipeline
loaded_pipeline = Pipeline.from_dict(config)
Component instances that hold non-serialisable state (open connections, GPU tensors) should implement
Haystack ships an evaluation framework for RAG quality metrics — faithfulness (is the answer grounded in the context?) and answer similarity.

from haystack.components.evaluators import FaithfulnessEvaluator, SASEvaluator

faithfulness_eval = FaithfulnessEvaluator()
sas_eval = SASEvaluator(model="cross-encoder/stsb-roberta-base")
sas_eval.warm_up()

questions = ["Where is the Eiffel Tower?"]
contexts = [["The Eiffel Tower is located in Paris, France."]]
responses = ["The Eiffel Tower is in Paris."]
ground_truths = ["Paris, France"]

faith_result = faithfulness_eval.run(
    questions=questions, contexts=contexts, responses=responses
)
sas_result = sas_eval.run(
    predicted_answers=responses, ground_truth_answers=ground_truths
)
print(f"Faithfulness: {faith_result['individual_scores']}")
print(f"SAS: {sas_result['individual_scores']}")
Comparing Haystack to Other RAG Frameworks
Combine a BM25 keyword retriever with a semantic embedding retriever and merge their results for improved recall.

from haystack.components.retrievers.in_memory import (
    InMemoryBM25Retriever,
    InMemoryEmbeddingRetriever,
)
from haystack.components.joiners import DocumentJoiner

pipeline = Pipeline()
pipeline.add_component("bm25", InMemoryBM25Retriever(document_store=store, top_k=5))
pipeline.add_component("embedder", SentenceTransformersTextEmbedder(model="BAAI/bge-small-en-v1.5"))
pipeline.add_component("semantic", InMemoryEmbeddingRetriever(document_store=store, top_k=5))
pipeline.add_component("joiner", DocumentJoiner(join_mode="reciprocal_rank_fusion"))
pipeline.add_component("prompt_builder", PromptBuilder(template=prompt_template))
pipeline.add_component("llm", OpenAIGenerator(model="gpt-4o-mini"))

pipeline.connect("embedder.embedding", "semantic.query_embedding")
pipeline.connect("bm25.documents", "joiner.documents")
pipeline.connect("semantic.documents", "joiner.documents")
pipeline.connect("joiner.documents", "prompt_builder.documents")
pipeline.connect("prompt_builder.prompt", "llm.prompt")
Large pipelines that contain I/O-heavy components (LLM calls, database queries) benefit from async execution.

import asyncio

async def main():
    result = await pipeline.run_async({
        "embedder": {"text": "What is the capital of France?"},
        "prompt_builder": {"question": "What is the capital of France?"},
    })
    print(result["llm"]["replies"][0])

asyncio.run(main())
Haystack 2.x builds RAG and agentic pipelines as typed DAGs where components connect via named, type-checked input/output sockets.
; pipelines validate connections at build time, preventing type errors before any data flows.
for development; swap to Qdrant, Weaviate, Elasticsearch, or pgvector for production.
